title: Katherine Johnson
ground_truth_corrupted_sentence: The space agency noted her "historical role as one of the first African-American women to work as a NASA engineer".
guessed_corrupted_sentence: The space agency noted her "historical role as one of the first African-American women to work as a NASA engineer".

parsed_response.corrupted_sentence: The space agency noted her "historical role as one of the first African-American women to work as a NASA engineer".
parsed_response.explanation: Katherine Johnson was a mathematician (often called a "human computer"), not an engineer. NASA highlights her as a pioneering mathematician/scientist. The milestone of becoming NASA’s first African-American female engineer belongs to Mary W. Jackson. Thus labeling Johnson as an engineer in this context is incorrect and conflates her achievements with Jackson’s.
